text: The PR addresses 46 performance audit issues across 33 files with generally sensible fixes including memoization, dynamic imports, targeted watch() calls, and component extraction. However, the ProtectedRoute.tsx fix introduces an authorization bypass vulnerability (string.includes vs array.includes), and several critical audit findings (HoursBarChart.tsx barrel import, Sidebar.tsx icon imports, CategoryPieChart.tsx tree-shaking) remain unaddressed. Additionally, four landing page components lost their translate-y animation due to replacing transition-all with transition-opacity alone.

reasoning: Critical authorization bypass in ProtectedRoute.tsx (string.includes over array.includes allows role substring collisions), plus missing coverage of several audit findings and introduced animation regressions. Must fix before merge.

message: Well-structured extraction of VolunteerRow into a memoized component with explicit props interface. This properly addresses the re-render issue and improves code organization.
ToolbarButton extracted to module scope with React.memo, and all toolbar handlers wrapped in useCallback. Eliminates anonymous function recreation on every keystroke.
Cleanly removed inline ThemeToggleButton definition (which caused remounting on every render) and replaced with the existing ThemeToggle component. Eliminates a critical performance bug.
Replaced blanket watch() with targeted individual field watches and properly scoped useMemo dependencies. Fixes unnecessary re-renders and makes memoization effective.
Fixed mutation feedback to use the variables parameter from onSuccess instead of dereferencing mutation.variables, which could have been stale.
Dates pre-formatted via useMemo, eliminating redundant new Date() calls on every render for each transaction.
Dates pre-formatted via useMemo, eliminating redundant new Date() calls on every render for each organization.
EventSeriesForm imported via next/dynamic with ssr:false, keeping ~440 lines of form logic out of the initial bundle.
RichTextEditor imported via next/dynamic with ssr:false, keeping all of @tiptap out of the initial bundle unless the blog form is opened.
Added duplicate guard to addCertification preventing blank or duplicate certification entries, and used index-based keys to avoid React key collisions.

Authorization bypass: rolesKey (string from allowedRoles.join(',')) uses String.prototype.includes() instead of Array.prototype.includes(). Since 'ORGANIZATION_ADMIN' contains 'ADMIN' as a substring, a user with role 'ADMIN' can bypass the ORGANIZATION_ADMIN guard at app/(org-admin)/layout.tsx:20 and access organization-admin routes. This is a direct consequence of the fix — the original array .includes() was correct, just needed dependency stabilization.
suggestion: Revert to Array.prototype.includes() for authorization checks. Stabilize the dependency by using a string key like JSON.stringify(allowedRoles) or wrapping in useMemo in parent components. Example: const rolesKey = JSON.stringify(allowedRoles); ... allowedRoles.includes(user.role); with rolesKey in the dependency array. Or use useDeepCompareEffect.

message: Critical audit finding not addressed: barrel import of recharts components (Bar, BarChart, CartesianGrid, ResponsiveContainer, Tooltip, XAxis, YAxis) from top-level 'recharts' prevents tree-shaking, adding ~500KB to bundle. PR claims to fix 6 critical issues but this one was skipped entirely.
suggestion: Use next/dynamic with ssr:false to import the chart dynamically, and import recharts from specific subpaths (e.g. recharts/es6/bar/Bar, recharts/es6/cartesian/BarChart) to enable tree-shaking.

message: Critical audit finding only partially addressed. The useMemo for chartData was added (good), but the barrel import of 6 recharts components from top-level 'recharts' still prevents tree-shaking. The audit specifically called for both next/dynamic and specific subpath imports.
suggestion: Apply the same pattern: use next/dynamic for the chart component and import recharts from specific subpaths to enable tree-shaking.

message: Transition regression: motion-safe:transition-all replaced with motion-safe:transition-opacity, but the component also animates translate-y (transform). Without transition-transform, the slide-up animation jumps instantly instead of smoothly transitioning. Affects CTA.tsx, ImpactStats.tsx:19, LevelPathway.tsx:47, MissionVision.tsx:31 — all four landing scroll-reveal sections.
suggestion: Add transition-transform: motion-safe:transition-[opacity,transform] motion-safe:duration-700, or revert to transition-all since only opacity and transform are actually used (the audit's premise was correct but the suggested fix was incomplete).

message: Important audit finding not addressed: 19 individual Lucide icon imports bound into the ICONS object map. All icons are bundled even if only a subset is used by a given role's navItems.
suggestion: Use dynamic icon access (import * as Icons from 'lucide-react' and Icons[item.icon]) or code-split nav item definitions per role.

message: Date formatting not memoized: new Date(u.createdAt).toLocaleDateString('en-IN', ...) runs on every render for each user row. The same pattern was fixed in PointsHistory.tsx and OrganizationTable.tsx via useMemo but was missed here.
suggestion: Apply the same pattern: pre-format dates via useMemo keyed on users array, then reference the formatted value in the template.

message: Inline date formatting not memoized: new Date(req.requestedAt).toLocaleDateString('en-IN', ...) runs on every render for each approval request in the queue.
suggestion: Pre-format dates via useMemo similarly to PointsHistory.tsx and OrganizationTable.tsx.

message: Added will-change-transform to decorative blurred background divs. While this promotes to compositor layers (reducing paint cost), will-change creates a new compositor layer for each element, consuming GPU memory. For ephemeral celebration overlays this is acceptable, but the strategy should be consistent — consider using will-change only during the fade-in animation and removing it after.
suggestion: Use a micro-library approach: add will-change before animation starts (via useEffect) and remove it after animation completes using transitionend or setTimeout.

message: Removed disableTransitionOnChange. Per the audit, this was done because it 'suppresses CSS transitions during theme swap to prevent FOUC, but can cause jarring instant flash instead of smooth transition.' However, without disableTransitionOnChange, the theme swap may now show intermediate states (the very FOUC it was designed to prevent). This is a trade-off the audit itself flagged as potentially worse.
suggestion: Reinstate disableTransitionOnChange if FOUC is observed during theme switching. The original behavior was intentional.
